title: Campium undulatum
taxonomy: Campium undulatum is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. Campium undulatum is commonly known as the undulate campium fern. This fern is typically found growing in shaded, humid environments. Campium undulatum is a relatively rare species that is often overlooked in its native habitats.
uses: Campium undulatum, a species of flowering plant, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat digestive problems and as an anti-inflammatory agent. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of Campium undulatum's medicinal uses.
